parts.instructions: For each question, choose the correct answer.

content.message: Harry, We're out of milk. We'll need some tonight, so can you stop somewhere on your way home from college? Dinner is at 6. Mum

questions.question: What is the message about?

options.A: Harry's mother is finding out what Harry wants for dinner.
options.B: Harry's mother is telling Harry not to be late home tonight.
options.C: Harry's mother is asking Harry to buy something for her.

questions.explanation: The mother asks Harry to 'stop somewhere on your way home' to get milk because they're 'out of milk'.

content.message: The movie at the film club tonight is very popular. So shall I see you at our usual meeting place, but a bit earlier?

questions.question: What does Tom want to change?

options.B: the place where he'll meet Sammy
options.C: the movie he and Sammy will see

questions.explanation: Tom wants to meet 'at our usual meeting place' but 'a bit earlier' due to the movie being popular.

content.title: Harwood College Picnic
content.details:
Saturday 1 p.m.
Sports field (Gym if raining)
Harwood students – free
Guests – £5
Fantastic band!
Food included

questions.question: What does the notice say?

options.A: Everyone who comes to the picnic should bring some food.
options.B: The picnic won't happen if the weather is bad.
options.C: Some people who come to the picnic must pay.

questions.explanation: The notice says 'Guests – £5', meaning guests must pay. Students are free, and the picnic moves to the gym if raining.

content.message: I'm driving to college every day this term. If you can pay a bit for petrol, I'll take you too. Parking's free.

questions.question: Carol wants Emily

options.A: to share the cost of driving to college.
options.B: to give her a lift to college some days.
options.C: to help pay for parking at college.

questions.explanation: Carol says 'If you can pay a bit for petrol, I'll take you too', meaning Emily should contribute to fuel costs.

content.title: City Station
content.details:
Bad weather!
Trains working as usual
Be careful of ice on platforms

questions.question: What is the problem today?

options.C: The station might need to close.

questions.explanation: The notice warns about ice on platforms, suggesting passengers should be careful to avoid falling.

content.message: Any ideas for Frank's birthday present? I can get something at the shopping centre on my way to his party on Saturday.

questions.question: What is Liz doing in this text message?
questions.explanation: Liz asks 'Any ideas for Frank's birthday present?' which is asking for advice/suggestions.

parts.instructions: For each question, choose the correct answer.

parts.instructions: For each question, choose the correct answer.

passage.title: A lucky teenager
passage.text:
Fifteen-year-old British student Jonathan Davis has always loved learning about the past. So, when he saw a newspaper article about people who enjoy looking in the countryside for very old things made of metal, such as coins, he wanted to try the hobby himself.

To get started, he had to have something called a detector. You hold this machine over the ground, and it makes a loud noise if there is metal there. He bought a small one and began using it near the village where he lives.

He didn't find much for the first few months. Then, one day, he and a friend were looking in a field. 'We were a bit bored,' he says. 'But suddenly, I found a few bits of metal lying in the grass. As soon as I saw them, I had a feeling they might be really old, so I showed them to my friend. We kept looking, and together we found 200 more pieces that day!'

Jonathan took the pieces to a museum. The staff there told him they were almost two thousand years old. They explained that at that time people sometimes cut silver bowls and cups into small pieces, and then used the pieces as money.

The museum later had a special exhibition of the silver pieces, which Jonathan went to. He said, 'It was really exciting to see what the people at the museum were able to do to clean the pieces of silver and find out exactly what they are. They look really different now. I'm so pleased I found them and that now other people can see them too.'

questions.question: What do we learn about Jonathan from the first paragraph?

options.B: He has lots of unusual hobbies.

questions.explanation: The first paragraph states Jonathan 'has always loved learning about the past' which shows his interest in history.

questions.question: What is the writer doing in the second paragraph?
questions.explanation: The second paragraph explains how a metal detector works: 'You hold this machine over the ground, and it makes a loud noise if there is metal there.'

questions.question: What does Jonathan say in the third paragraph about the metal pieces?

options.B: It took several days to find all of them.
options.C: He was pleased when his friend found them.

questions.explanation: Jonathan says 'I had a feeling they might be really old' when he first saw them, indicating he found them interesting.

questions.question: What did the staff at the museum tell Jonathan about the metal pieces?

options.A: They were not as old as Jonathan thought.
options.B: It's a shame they were so small.
options.C: In the past, people used them to buy and sell things.

questions.explanation: The staff explained that 'people sometimes cut silver bowls and cups into small pieces, and then used the pieces as money.'

questions.question: How did Jonathan feel when he visited the exhibition?

options.A: surprised so many people came to see the silver
options.B: happy he could share the silver with others
options.C: sad that the silver looked so different

questions.explanation: Jonathan said 'I'm so pleased I found them and that now other people can see them too', showing he was happy to share.

parts.instructions: For each question, choose the correct answer.

passage.title: Toni Morrison
passage.text_with_gaps:
The African-American writer Toni Morrison was born in 1931. When she was growing up, her parents (19)......... her about African-American history and music. She often said that (20)......... stories was an important (21)......... of her family's daily life. She also loved books and spent a lot of her free time reading.

In 1949, Toni Morrison went to Howard University in Washington, D.C. to study English. After (22)......... her course, her first job was teaching. She wrote her first book, The Bluest Eye, in 1970. Her later books were very popular, (23)......... Beloved, which is now a well-known film. Toni Morrison (24)......... many national and international prizes, including the Nobel Prize for Literature in 1993. She became the first black woman ever to receive this.

questions.gap_context: When she was growing up, her parents ______ her about African-American history and music.
questions.explanation: 'Taught' is correct as parents teach their children about history and culture.

questions.gap_context: She often said that ______ stories was an important part of her family's daily life.
questions.explanation: 'Telling stories' is the correct collocation for narrating stories.

questions.gap_context: ...was an important ______ of her family's daily life.
questions.explanation: 'Part' is correct as it means a component or element of something.

questions.gap_context: After ______ her course, her first job was teaching.
questions.explanation: 'Finishing' a course means completing one's studies.

questions.gap_context: Her later books were very popular, ______ Beloved, which is now a well-known film.
questions.explanation: 'Especially' is used to single out one person, thing, or situation.

questions.gap_context: Toni Morrison ______ many national and international prizes, including the Nobel Prize for Literature in 1993.
questions.explanation: 'Won prizes' is the correct collocation for receiving awards.

parts.instructions: For each question, write ONE word for each gap.

passage.context: Email from Marcia to Annie about starting a basketball team
passage.text_with_gaps:
I am trying (0) to start a girls' basketball team at college. Are you interested (25)......... joining it? Basketball's (26)......... excellent way to get exercise, and it can also be a (27)......... of fun.

Our sports teacher, Mrs Thompson, says she will be our coach, but only (28)......... we can get enough girls for a team. We will probably practise once or twice a week in the college gym.

(29)......... you know any other girls we can ask to be on the team? Let me know as soon (30)......... possible so we can begin practising.

questions.explanation: 'Interested in' is a fixed prepositional phrase.

questions.gap_context: Basketball's ______ excellent way to get exercise...
questions.explanation: Article 'an' is needed before 'excellent way' (excellent starts with a vowel sound).

questions.gap_context: ...and it can also be a ______ of fun.
questions.explanation: 'A lot of' or 'a load of' are common expressions meaning 'much/many'.

questions.gap_context: ...but only ______ we can get enough girls for a team.
questions.explanation: 'If' introduces a conditional clause; 'when' is also acceptable as it refers to time condition.

questions.gap_context: ______ you know any other girls we can ask to be on the team?
questions.explanation: 'Do' is used to form questions in the present simple tense.

questions.gap_context: Let me know as soon ______ possible...
questions.explanation: 'As soon as' is a fixed expression meaning 'whenever' or 'immediately when'.

difficulty_scale.2: Medium - Some inference and vocabulary knowledge required
difficulty_scale.3: Hard - Complex grammar, idioms, or inference required
